shortlist.one_liner: Apple Watch app that tracks heart rate recovery between sets and suggests optimal rest periods
shortlist.problem_statement: Gym-goers either rest too long (wasting time) or too short (risking injury). No app uses real-time HR data to personalize rest periods.
shortlist.target_user: Regular gym-goers who wear Apple Watch during workouts

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: STRONG (6 weeks — HealthKit + WatchKit + simple UI)
feasibility.monetization_viability: STRONG (subscription $3.99/mo — fitness users pay for tools)
feasibility.competition_density: STRONG (few apps focus specifically on HR-based rest timing)

shortlist.monetization_model: Freemium — free with 3 workouts/week, $3.99/mo unlimited + insights
shortlist.competition_notes: Strong Timer+ and Intervals Pro exist but focus on pre-set timers, not adaptive HR-based rest
shortlist.mvp_scope: Watch app with HR monitoring during rest, vibration alert when ready, basic iOS companion for history
shortlist.next_step: Run the product-agent skill with idea: 'Apple Watch app that tracks heart rate recovery between sets and suggests optimal rest periods based on real-time HR data' for watchOS + iOS

shortlist.one_liner: Track steps, route, and calories during meetings — share walking meeting summaries with attendees
shortlist.problem_statement: Walking meetings are popular for health but there's no way to track the health benefit or share it. Calendar apps don't connect to HealthKit.
shortlist.target_user: Knowledge workers and managers who take walking meetings

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: STRONG (5 weeks — HealthKit + MapKit + Calendar integration)
feasibility.competition_density: EXCELLENT (no dedicated walking meeting app exists)
feasibility.technical_fit: STRONG (HealthKit skills transfer, MapKit is new but manageable)

shortlist.monetization_model: One-time purchase $4.99 with optional $1.99/mo for team features
shortlist.competition_notes: Pedometer apps exist but none integrate with calendar or frame walking as meetings
shortlist.mvp_scope: Start/stop walking meeting, auto-detect from calendar, log steps + route, share summary
shortlist.next_step: Run the product-agent skill with idea: 'iOS app that tracks steps, route, and calories during walking meetings and shares summaries with attendees' for iOS + watchOS

shortlist.one_liner: Crowdsourced gym equipment availability — see which machines are free before you go
shortlist.problem_statement: Gyms are crowded at peak hours. No way to know if the squat rack is free without going. Leads to wasted time and frustration.
shortlist.target_user: Gym members at commercial gyms (Planet Fitness, LA Fitness, Equinox)

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: MODERATE (8 weeks — needs crowdsource mechanics and gym database)
feasibility.platform_api_fit: MODERATE (MapKit for gym location, but core logic is custom)
feasibility.competition_density: STRONG (no quality solution exists — GymBook is workout logging, not availability)
feasibility.technical_fit: MODERATE (needs backend for crowdsource data — outside current skills)

shortlist.monetization_model: Freemium — free for 1 gym, $2.99/mo for multiple gyms + predictions
shortlist.competition_notes: Some gyms have their own capacity apps but no cross-gym equipment-level tracking exists
shortlist.mvp_scope: Single gym support, manual check-in/check-out for equipment, peak time predictions
shortlist.next_step: Run the product-agent skill with idea: 'Crowdsourced gym equipment availability app that shows which machines are free at your gym' for iOS

ideas_filtered_out.reason: Extremely competitive (Fitbod, Future, Hevy). Would need 6+ months to differentiate. Failed solo_dev_scope filter.
Apple's own sleep tracking in watchOS 10+ covers most of this. Failed competition_density filter — competing with platform owner.

recommendation: Start with Rank 1 (Workout Recovery Timer). It scores highest (8.4/10), leverages your exact skills (HealthKit + Core Motion), has a clear monetization path ($3.99/mo subscription), and can ship in 6 weeks. Run the product-agent skill to validate the problem before committing.
